title: Masks N Glory
description: a mod originally made for my minecraft smp, it adds some weapons, some masks, magic and bring back the Farlands
body:
![Masks N Glory](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/588ddaa8dbabd66b4cc94bcebef55168417a6e43.png)

## "This Mod adds some weapons with special enchants and abilities, Masks with helpful attributes, blocks for decoration, weird foods, and more!"
  
**"You will need to sail accross the world, explore temples and dungeons, new worlds, old worlds, and Far Lands, to find the pieces of broken artifacts. Fight armies and get ressources to save your friends from limbo. Or instead, travels into the deep, experiment with ancient shards, craft new materials, and eventually figure out a way to upgrade what you never imagined could be upgraded any further....
or maybe you just want to make soup and candies for your friends, who knows."**
  
**"If you want alternatives for vanilla helmets, try the new masks, they're lightweight, stylish, and some of them come with some sort of abilities."**


  _this mod, was made for my minecraft SMP, some weapons are direct references to a bunch of different medias, 50000 blocks out, it also re-add the "Farlands" to the game, a modified version with a bunch of lore and changes. The mod is meant to be played with "enchancement" and might seem unbalanced without it._



![Special masks and their shards](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8848346a700f9256a70183ce477506c377fe1061.png)

### some more informations:

**The Masks:**

<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Ego Mask: running towards your opponent to hit them will knockback them away, dealing a bit more damage than normal
   
2. Happy Mask "Fool's Luck": gives "sugar rush", "hero of the village" and "luck" when worn, but gives "bad luck", "wither" and "slowness" when taken off.
   
3. Grinning Mask "Sadist": Deal bonus damage based on how healthy the target is (also keep bad omen).
   
4. Sad Mask "Grief Pulse": When you take damage, emit a burst of slowness 2 to all enemies within 5 blocks.
   
5. Knight Mask "Last Stand": Dropping below 4 hearts triggers Strength 3 and Speed2 for 6 seconds. 60 second cooldown.
   
6. Eye Mask "Third Eye": Entities within 12 blocks are highlighted through walls.
   
7. Null Mask "dimension shift": teleport to the nether roof by sneaking, and teleport back to the world spawn by sneaking again, 2min cooldown for every tp.
   
8. Oji Mask "Iron Shell": First hit in any encounter is reduced by 80%. Resets after 4 seconds without taking damage.
   
9. Piko Mask "Lifesteal": Heal for 15% of all melee damage dealt.
   
10. Tog Mask "Golden Everything": You can eat at full hunger. Eating any food gives a 4 second strength 1 burst.
   
11. HS Mask "fuckass guy": Fall damage is converted into a shockwave which knocks nearby enemies away from the landing point.
   
12. DogSout Mask "Mr. President": If another player wearing any mask is within 10 blocks, both of you deal +2 damage to shared targets. (also keep speed 4)
   
13. Corv Mask "Kalzium's fall": You always see the health bar of any entity you're looking at. Arrows aimed at you deal 30% less damage.
   
14. Crash Mask "Rampage": While sprinting, hitting an enemy creates a small explosion.
   
15. Hound Mask "Bloodhound": Your last attacker is always highlighted through walls. Damage you deal increases by 10% for each missing heart on the target.
   
16. Dave Mask "Second Wind": Once per 2 minutes, surviving a killing blow leaves you at 1 heart. While below 4 hearts, speed is passively boosted.
   
17. Stonei Mask "Fortress": Standing still for 3 seconds builds up to 40% damage reduction. Any movement resets it instantly.
   
18. D-Man Mask "Dark Cartoon": Your attacks apply a stacking debuff; each hit makes the target take +5% more damage from all sources (up to 5 stacks).

</details>

**The foods:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Gilded Soup: a good alternative to golden carrot, it gives a good ammount of saturation and food, and unlike other stews it can stack to 64
   
2. Echo Caramel: a candy made from echo shards, it give low food points, but big saturation points, and can be eaten when not hungry. When eaten, it will give the "sugar rush" effect.
   
3. Choglowberry: A chocolate made with glowberries, it's a gamble, it gives low saturation and low food points, BUT, it can be eaten when not hungry, and can give 1 effect out of 5 (I think), one of them being saturation, which will max your saturation instantly; It also has a higher chance to make you glow.

4. Echo Caramel Apple: an apple covered in Echo Caramel, it gives the same boost as the said candy, just slightly longer. (also gives a bit more food and saturation than a normal apple)

5. Echo Caramel Golden apple: a golden apple covered in Echo Caramel, it gives the same boost as the normal golden apple, just slightly longer and with the boost of the echo caramel on top. (also gives a bit more food and saturation than a normal golden apple)

6. Pale apple: a variant of the enchanted golden apple, made not for combat, but for escapes. It grants you with invisibility, speed 7, resistance 1, fire resistance AND weakness 7, as it's not meant to be an offensive buff, but a way to quickly avoid fights and simply attempt a... Tactical retreat.

</details>

**The Weapons:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Pale Bone Alloy Saber: a direct upgrade of the netherite sword, a slightly weaker damage output, with a higher speed, custom sounds, and 3 custom enchantments.

2. Golden Warpan: an ancient artifact, a collector item, made for a dear friend of mine, this weapon's damage output might be low, but try gainning some height and you'll see just how devastating its slam attacks are.
be careful however, each time you succefully land a slam attack, you'll dent the weapon a bit more, making its damage output weaker on the slams until it fixes itself.

3. Pale Steel Greatsword: a direct upgrade for the netherite axe. It keeps the same attack damage and speed, it also gains the ability to be thrown and come back to the person's hand, dealing damage when thrown and also on its way back. On top of that it also gets 4 custom enchantments.

4. Bone Alloy Knife: an insanely fast but low damage weapon, like the others it has custom hit sounds and 2 custom enchantments, of course, there's something more to it; do you know what a "feedbacker" is ? no? fine I'll explain. This knife can, if timed right, parry any and all projectile coming your way, sending it back with tenfold the power to its original owner.
this isn't all of course; you can also hold it in your offhand, and instead parry your own arrows to boost them even faster toward an enemy.

5. Pale Glaive: A terrible weapon. Only deals 1 damage, and its speed is average at best.
maybe there's something more to it.

6. Prideful Husk: An upgrade to the Pale Bone Alloy Saber, it looses some speed, but gains the ability to coat itself with any potion you hold in your offhand by right clicking the sword. Giving you 3 uses of the potion effect when striking an opponent.

7. Pale Steel Coins: throw your pocket change in the air, and shoot it with an arrow to ricochet the arrow around every coins you've thrown and auto aim at the enemy with insane damage output.

8. Nulled Knife: a mysterious knife made of an unknown alloy. It seems like the original is lost to time. Whenever you hit someone with it, you give them the "null" effect, turning 2 random slots in their inventory into an "error null" item, this lasts for 10 second before the slots gets turned back, the knife goes on cooldown for 30.

9. Pale War-Pickaxe: a pickaxe with good attack and speed stats, can be turned into a crossbow and fire shears at opponent like a minigun, this will inflict bleeding.

</details>


**The Armor:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. While Worst than diamond (protection wise), the pale steel armor, has a pretty strong ability. When worn fully, every and all effect you get will have its level upped by 1.

   - while this can be really strong if you carry potions, it can be double edged, if an opponent has a way to deal debuffs to you, they will also be upped by 1.

   - you also get a new effect called actor, while worn.

2. The "Golden Retribution Helmet" is an upgrade to the Pale Helmet, if you make it, you loose the whole potion thing with full armor, but gain a new ability. By wearing the helmet and taking damage, you build up charge, and once fully charged, you can press a keybind (V by default) to create a flashbang and blind everyone in a 10 blocks radius for 5 second.

</details>



**The Enchants:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Atychiphobia: (only for pale saber/prideful husk) give the opponent weakness 1 on hit

2. Discombobulated: (only for greatsword) makes the opponent slower on hit

3. Blunt: (only for greatsword) make the opponent blind on hit

4. Disoriented: (only for warpan) give the opponent extreme nausea on hit
   
5. Thrill: (only for pale saber/prideful husk) whenever you start a combo you'll slowly build up additional damage and heal yourself, scaling with how long a combo has been going on for.

6. Guillotine: (only for pale saber/prideful husk) If the opponent is under 4.5 hearts and are trying to: "use an item" which includes "eating food" using endearpearls to escape, or more, you can hit them to intterupt that action and they will get executed.

7. Skull Breaker: (only for warpan) Deals additional damage scaling with blocks fallen.

9. Combo: (only for knife) attempt to hit your opponent as much as you can before they die, once they do, the number of hits you landed will turn into absorption hearts.

10. Antiseptic: (only for knife) when holding the knife, you become immune to: poison, wither, slowness, weakness, blindness.

11. Legion of The Undead: (only for glaive) Apply the hunger effect on hit for a short duration.

12. Retribution: (only for the golden retribution helmet) Summon evoker fangs all around you in an aoe instead of the flashbang.

13. Vexed: (only for the golden retribution helmet) Summon 7 vexes around you to aid you in battle instead of the flashbang, it will also apply "Spite" to anyone in the radius.

14. Homing: (only for Greatsword): you get plus 20 blocks radius on your sword throw, it can auto aim at a target if you've hit them beforehand and on top of that it gives the "pinned" effect to the opponent, making sure they can't fly away anymore. In exchange you loose half the damage on a sword throw and get a 25 second cooldown every throw.

15. Remorse: (only for Greatsword): if you throw your sword in an entity, it now embed into them, you need to right click to get it back, dealing a bit of damage on the way back and applying the "Bleeding" effect, making your target slowly bleed out. you can also sneak and right click to tp to them and snap the sword out of their bodies, dealing a bit more damage but not applying bleeding.

16. Marrow: (only for saber): increase the sword's damage output scaling with how much armor pieces you're NOT wearing.

17. Lockout: (only for saber): every 6 hits, you apply the "lockout" effect."

18. Incumbent: (only for prideful husk): damage scale up the longer a fight has been going on for, it resets after both parties stop fighting.

19. Temper: (only for prideful husk): the longer you wait before attacking your opponent without swinging your blade, the more damage you'll do.

20. Notoriety: (only for prideful husk): every player you kill will give a 0.5 damage boost to your sword. Only work once per player and reset on death.

21. Afterlife: (only for the glaive): kill mobs/players to store soul energy in your glaive and right click to shoot homing soul bullets. some mobs have special interaction with your glaive when killed.

22. Risk: (only for the glaive): apply "rotting" on hit.

</details>


**The Effects:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Sugar Rush: shake your screen, hide your health bar and make you faster, the effects get stronger the higher the effect is. You might feel tired after the rush ends.

2. Rot: Hide your health bar, replacing it with empty heart texture.

3. Spite: the mob or player with this effect will instantly be the target of ANY neutral or hostile mobs

4. Pinned: slames the player with that effect down on the ground every 2 ticks they're in the air. also prevent elytra flight and trident riptide.

5. Actor: A player with that effect, if watched by an audience during fights, will have a part of the damage they received be transfered over to the audience instead of receiving it fully.

6. Off-script: turns you and your armor invisible aswell as making you immune to projectiles.

7. Stunt double: with that effect, any totem you pop will be ignored and you will die normally.

8. Seized: prevent a player to sprint or jump.

9. Bleeding: a slow but constant damage flow that does not trigger "damage ticking".

</details>




**Unknown facts:**


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

1. Echo shards can now be farmed, they have a 50% chance of dropping when breaking sculk.
2. The shards for EGO's mask can drop at 1% from wither skeletons.
3. The shards for HS's mask can drop at 0.1% from dark oak logs.
4. The shards for Dman's mask can drop at 1% from sea pickles.
5. The Pale Bone Alloy Saber hides your nametag when held.
6. The ERROR:NULL has a 1%, 2% and 3% chance to appear in certain stronghold chests
7. The Golden Scrap drops at 2% from evokers
8. The Gilded Heart if eaten in hardcore, will revive anybody who died and is currently spectating the game. If multiple people are spectating, it will choose one at random.

</details>


**_Note: This mod was made with Enchancement in mind, most of the enchants will work a lot better when used alongside it._**



    

_Thank you @InfinityFarzad (https://modrinth.com/user/InfinityFarzad) for the "Pale Steel Greatsword" texture!_

_And of course, thank you @Ironfist4657 (https://github.com/jayden-deason/Soulforged) for the WHOLE code for the ability of the "Prideful Husk"_
